subprocessors.name: Amazon Web Services
services: Amazon Web Services (AWS) provides the cloud infrastructure on which the Twenty CRM runs — compute, managed databases, object storage, and encrypted backups. AWS is Twenty's primary hosting provider.

subprocessors.name: Anthropic
services: Anthropic provides large language models that power Twenty's optional AI features, such as drafting, summarization, and data enrichment.

subprocessors.name: ClickHouse
services: ClickHouse provides the analytical database Twenty uses to store audit logs and power in-product analytics and reporting.

subprocessors.name: Cloudflare
services: Cloudflare provides Twenty's content delivery network, DNS, and web application firewall, securing and accelerating traffic to the service. It does not retain Customer Personal Data on a long-term basis.

subprocessors.name: Google Cloud Platform
services: Google Cloud Platform provides specific Google services that Twenty integrates, such as Maps and Google sign-in.

subprocessors.name: Mistral
services: Mistral AI provides large language models that power Twenty's optional AI features. The AI provider used depends on the model configured for the workspace.

subprocessors.name: OpenAI
services: OpenAI provides large language models that power Twenty's optional AI features. The AI provider used depends on the model configured for the workspace.

subprocessors.name: Sentry
services: Sentry provides application error and performance monitoring, helping Twenty detect, diagnose, and resolve issues in the service.
